hypothesis: If nudged elastic band calculations are executed on an iteratively fine-tuned EquiformerV2 surrogate within a Bayesian Algorithm Execution loop, then minimum-energy paths for LJ7 and LJ38 transitions will reach ground-truth convergence thresholds (e.g., fmax) with comparable accuracy but using substantially fewer expensive simulator evaluations than classical NEB, because surrogate-guided NEB concentrates acquisitions along the true MEP and transition-state regions.
